rulings.Case Title: VANNOY MCCREE VS. CHEN ZHPU HENG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug. 29, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT MICHAEL HSU's MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC. GRANTED. Moving party carried its burden under Tech-Built. No opposition filed. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Noticeof Motion And Motion To Enforce The Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug. 29, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFF MASAHIRO SEKINE's Motion To Enforce The Settlement Agreement OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service of the moving papers on Defendant's counsel of record on file. Defendant, represented by counsel, was to be personally served by no later than August 15, 2022 per the order shortening time. No proof of service on file reflects personal service on counsel of record, i.e. proper service on a represented party. If moving party is in possession of a filed-endorsed copy of a proof of service on counsel of record filed (or submitted for filing) at least 5 court days before August 29, 2022, moving party shall present a copy of such proof of service to the courtroom clerk no later than 9:00 a.m. on August 29, 2021. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WILMINGTON SAVINGS FUND SOCIETY, FSB VS. EMIL P MILYAKOV ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reclassify Case
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug. 29, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT NEVENNA APOSTOLOVA's Motion To Reclassify Case GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to reclassify the action from limited to unlimited jurisdiction is granted. Motion to reclassify this action from an unlawful detainer to a general civil action is denied. No authority is cited for the proposition that an unlawful detainer may be reclassified while possession remains an issue in the case. No evidence is presented that possession is no longer at issue. Asuncion v. Super. Ct. (1980) 108 Cal. App. 3d 141 and Martin-Bragg v. Moore (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 367 are not on point and involved two simultaneously pending actions. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UNITY HOMES, INC. VS. GREGORY GEETER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug. 29, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT GREGORY GEETER's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT moot in light of the ruling on the motion to strike. The Court notes that amended UD-101 still affirmatively alleges that a 15 Day Notice to Pay Rent or Quit was served on the defendants who are alleged in the complaint to be forcibly detaining the property. This affirmative allegation is made in paragraphs 6 and 7 even though the instructions in UD-101 paragraph 3(b) are: "(If no is checked, the further items that need to be completed are the signature and verification on page 5). "No" is checked in paragraph 3(b). Plaintiff chose to make these allegations (that are not required) and the Court must take these allegations as true. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UNITY HOMES, INC. VS. GREGORY GEETER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug. 29, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT GREGORY GEETER's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT GRANTED. Amendment was untimely. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that form UD-101 containing supplemental allegations to an unlawful detainer complaint is not subject to the 5-day deadline applicable to filing of the amended complaint after demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
